# SESSION BRIEF · BU-reactions — Quiet, multi-select reactions on posts

_Brief version: 1.0 · Author: Paul + Claude · Date: April 2026_
_Priority: Phase 2 demo-leverage. First feature after the demo
milestone (Phase 1 closed). Unblocks Scenario 3 in full and partially
re-paints the social fabric of the feed._
_Pairs with: ERD Slice 2 minimal (already merged); BU-comments Comments
(future, will reuse the Reaction primitive via `targetType`)._

---

## Objective

Members can react to a post with one or more emoji from a fixed
8-emoji set (🕯️🙏❤️💪🎯💕👍😢). Reactions are quiet, multi-select,
toggleable, and aggregate visibly on the post card. The system never
celebrates a reaction — no streaks, no counts shouting, no pings.
Per design-philosophy.md principles 1 (one-click) and 3 (no anxiety
amplification).

Success: David (Scenario 3) opens the feed, taps the reaction pill on
the Shabbat post, picks 🕯️ and 🙏, and the reactions appear on the
card with no further fuss.

---

## Scope

### Build in this session

**Decision record (must land before schema migration):**

- `docs/architecture/decision-log.md` — append `D047 · Reaction
  schema, fixed 8-emoji set, multi-select` (MODIFY, append-only)

**Schema:**

- `prisma/schema.prisma` (MODIFY — append `Reaction` model and
  `ReactionEmoji` enum; add `reactions Reaction[]` relation on `Post`
  and `User`. Do NOT modify any existing fields. Existing entities,
  enums, and indexes preserved exactly.)
- `prisma/migrations/<timestamp>_add_reactions/migration.sql`
  (generated by `npx prisma migrate dev --name add_reactions`)

**Service:**

- `server/services/reaction.ts` (new) — `toggleReaction`,
  `listReactionsForPost` (returns aggregated counts + the caller's
  own selections)
- `server/services/reaction.README.md` (new)

**Router:**

- `server/routers/reaction.ts` (new) — `add`, `remove`, `listForPost`
- `server/routers/_app.ts` (MODIFY — register `reaction` router under
  the existing namespace block; preserve all other entries)

**Validation:**

- `shared/validation/reaction.ts` (new) — Zod schemas for the three
  procedures plus the `ReactionEmoji` enum mirror

**Service hook for feed:**

- `server/services/post.ts` (MODIFY — `listPosts` returns each post
  with `reactions: { emoji: ReactionEmoji; count: number; mine: boolean }[]`
  alongside existing fields. Do NOT change existing return shape;
  add the new field at the end.)

**UI:**

- `components/ReactionPill.tsx` (new) — the always-visible pill on
  every post card showing aggregate reactions; tap opens the tray
- `components/ReactionTray.tsx` (new) — the 8-emoji picker that
  appears when the pill is tapped; tap an emoji toggles the
  caller's reaction
- `components/PostCard.tsx` (MODIFY — render `<ReactionPill />` in
  the card footer next to the AM button when present, or directly
  below the body when no AM button. Pass through `post.reactions`
  and `post.id`.)

**Audit:**

- `server/services/audit.ts` is NOT modified. The reaction service
  calls the existing `auditLog` writer with `action: 'reaction.add'`
  / `reaction.remove`, `entityType: 'reaction'`. The audit writer's
  shape is already there; this is consumption, not change.

**Tests:**

- `tests/integration/reaction-service.test.ts` (new) — toggle on /
  off / multi-select / not-twice; uses real DB per `tests/unit/...`
  precedent
- `tests/unit/reaction-router.test.ts` (new) — covers auth gate,
  Zod rejection of bad emoji, and the response shape contract
- `eslint-rules/canonical-areas.json` (MODIFY — add `reaction` area
  prefix; mirror the entry in `docs/process/testid-convention.md`)
- `docs/process/testid-convention.md` (MODIFY — add `reaction` row
  in the area table)

**READMEs:**

- `components/README.md` (MODIFY — list new components)
- `server/services/README.md` (MODIFY — list new service)
- `server/routers/README.md` (MODIFY — list new router)

**Analytics:**

- The `reaction_added` event is already specced in
  `docs/product/analytics-events.md` (line 133). The service layer
  fires it via the existing structured-log writer (see how BU-composer
  fires `post_created`). No changes to the analytics doc — it's the
  contract this BU consumes.

### Do NOT touch

- Any file not listed above
- The existing `Post` model fields, indexes, or relation onDeletes
- `PostType` enum / `PostVisibility` enum (no changes)
- `server/services/post.ts` `createPost` function (only `listPosts`
  is modified)
- `app/compose/` — composer is unchanged
- `components/PostForm.tsx`, `components/ActivistMailerField.tsx`,
  `components/FeedList.tsx` — no changes (FeedList simply forwards
  the reactions field through props)
- The existing six F-rules and F14 — no rule changes
- `app/dev/login/`, `app/page.tsx`, `app/feed/page.tsx` — no app-
  surface changes (feed page already calls `post.list`; the new
  `reactions` field flows through automatically)
- `docs/feature-spec/v0.5.docx` — frozen; updates land in v0.6

### Out of scope for this session

- **Reactions on comments.** The `reaction_added` analytics event
  carries `is_comment` (bool) — but Comments are BU-comments, not yet
  built. This BU's `Reaction.targetType` enum exists with one value
  (`post`) so the schema is forward-compatible, but no `comment`
  value is added.
- **Custom or seasonal emoji.** Fixed set of eight per parking-lot
  "Reaction taxonomy". Admin-configurable reactions = parking-lot.
- **Real-time updates.** A reaction added by user A is visible to
  user B only after B refreshes the feed. No WebSocket / polling /
  optimistic-broadcast.
- **Notifications.** Reactions never trigger a notification.
  Per design-philosophy.md principle 3.
- **Reaction history per user.** Aggregations are by post; no
  "your reactions" view.
- **Animations.** Tap feedback is the existing component-library
  press state. No bouncing, exploding, particle effects.
- **Streak counting / gamification.** Explicitly forbidden.

---

## Contracts

### Inputs consumed

- `Post` model from `prisma/schema.prisma` (read-only — only the
  relation back-ref is added on Post)
- `User` model — only the relation back-ref is added
- `auditLog` from `server/services/audit.ts` — call signature
  unchanged
- `ctx.user` from `server/routers/context.ts` — already shaped
- `authedProcedure` from `server/lib/trpc.ts` — already shaped
- Existing F-rules + F14 — every new file conforms to the lint
  ratchet

### Outputs produced

**Schema additions (the contract — needs D047):**

```prisma
enum ReactionEmoji {
  candle      // 🕯️ — vigil / remembrance / Shabbat
  pray        // 🙏 — prayer / appreciation / thanks
  heart       // ❤️ — solidarity / love
  strong      // 💪 — strength / determination
  target      // 🎯 — agreed / on-point
  sparkle     // 💕 — warmth / community
  thumbsup    // 👍 — acknowledged / understood
  sad         // 😢 — grief / sympathy
}

enum ReactionTargetType {
  post  // only value at MVP; comment lands with BU-comments
}

model Reaction {
  id String @id @default(uuid())

  userId String
  user   User   @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  targetType ReactionTargetType
  targetId   String              // For targetType=post, this is Post.id

  postId String?
  post   Post?   @relation(fields: [postId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  emoji ReactionEmoji

  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  @@unique([userId, targetType, targetId, emoji], name: "one_emoji_per_user_per_target")
  @@index([targetType, targetId])
  @@index([postId, emoji])
}
```

**Why the dual `targetId` + `postId`:** `targetType` + `targetId` is
the polymorphic key for the future when comments use the same
primitive. `postId` is a concrete FK so cascade-delete works today
and Prisma can express the relation. When BU-comments lands, a `commentId`
column joins them. Surface in open questions if a cleaner pattern
exists in the codebase.

**Router contract (the API):**

```ts
// server/routers/reaction.ts
reaction.add: authedProcedure
  .input(z.object({ postId: z.string().uuid(), emoji: ReactionEmojiSchema }))
  .mutation: returns { success: true } | throws TRPCError

reaction.remove: authedProcedure
  .input(z.object({ postId: z.string().uuid(), emoji: ReactionEmojiSchema }))
  .mutation: returns { success: true } | throws TRPCError

reaction.listForPost: publicProcedure
  .input(z.object({ postId: z.string().uuid() }))
  .query: returns { emoji: ReactionEmoji; count: number; mine: boolean }[]
```

`add` is idempotent — reacting twice with the same emoji is a no-op
(returns `{success: true}` either way). `remove` is also idempotent.
This matches the toggle semantics of the UI: tap an emoji you've
already picked → it's removed.

**Feed contract (the integration point):**

`listPosts` returns each post as

```ts
{
  ...existingFields,
  reactions: Array<{
    emoji: ReactionEmoji;
    count: number;
    mine: boolean;  // false for non-authed callers
  }>;
}
```

The array is sorted by `count` desc, then by `emoji` enum order
(stable). Empty array when no reactions exist.

---

## Acceptance criteria

### Functional

- [ ] Schema migration runs cleanly on a fresh DB and on a DB with
      existing seeded posts (no rows lost)
- [ ] `Reaction` model + both enums + indexes exist after migration
- [ ] D047 ADR appended to decision-log.md describing the schema
- [ ] `reaction.add` mutation creates a Reaction row, fires
      `reaction_added` analytics event, returns success
- [ ] `reaction.add` with the same `(user, post, emoji)` is idempotent
      — second call doesn't create a duplicate row
- [ ] `reaction.remove` deletes the matching row, fires nothing in
      analytics (per spec — only `add` is instrumented)
- [ ] `reaction.remove` is idempotent — removing a reaction that
      doesn't exist returns success
- [ ] `reaction.listForPost` returns aggregates with `mine: true` only
      for emoji the caller has reacted with
- [ ] `reaction.listForPost` works for unauthenticated callers
      (returns aggregates with `mine: false` everywhere)
- [ ] `post.list` returns `reactions` field populated for every post
- [ ] PostCard renders ReactionPill below the body (or next to AM
      button when present)
- [ ] Tapping the pill expands the ReactionTray with 8 emoji
- [ ] Tapping an emoji toggles the reaction (add if absent, remove if
      present); UI updates optimistically
- [ ] Two emoji selected by the same user appear as two distinct
      counts (1 candle + 1 pray, not "2")
- [ ] User's selected reactions render with a visible "you-picked"
      treatment (e.g. ring around the pill segment)
- [ ] Aggregate of 0 → pill renders empty / muted with just "React"
      affordance
- [ ] Aggregate of N>0 → pill renders the top emoji(s) inline with
      counts
- [ ] All testids on new interactive elements are well-formed
      (`reaction-...`) per F14

### Non-functional

- [ ] TypeScript compiles with zero errors, zero `any`, zero
      `@ts-ignore`
- [ ] All existing tests pass (`npm run test`)
- [ ] New tests pass (3+ integration, 5+ unit minimum)
- [ ] Lint passes (`npm run lint`) — every new file has `@build-unit
      BU-reactions` AND a meaningful `@spec` per F13
- [ ] Boundaries respected: services do not import from app/components;
      components do not import from server/db; per F06 boundaries plugin
- [ ] No PII in any log line touched by this BU (per F06 rule 3)
- [ ] Manual click-through of Scenario 3 (David / Shabbat post)
      completed in dev — multi-select tested, no celebration ping fired
- [ ] Commit message: `feat(reactions): BU-reactions — quiet
      multi-select reactions on posts`

### What "ready to merge" looks like

- All acceptance criteria pass
- F14 lint clean (every new interactive element has a testid)
- Manual scenario click-through screenshot or short video attached to
  the PR
- D047 entry in decision-log.md
- README files in `components/`, `server/services/`, `server/routers/`
  list the new artefacts
- Open-questions section populated with anything that came up during
  build

---

## Permission matrix

| Action                            | Public | Member | Author of post | Coordinator | Director |
| --------------------------------- | ------ | ------ | -------------- | ----------- | -------- |
| View aggregate reactions          | ✓ (public posts) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Add own reaction                  | —      | ✓      | ✓              | ✓           | ✓        |
| Remove own reaction               | —      | ✓      | ✓              | ✓           | ✓        |
| See *who* reacted (per-user list) | —      | —      | —              | —           | —        |
| Remove someone else's reaction    | —      | —      | —              | —           | —        |

**Notes:**

- Authors can react to their own posts (no special-case). Open
  question 2 below if this should be different.
- Per-user reaction lists are explicitly not exposed in MVP. The
  router does not have a "who reacted" procedure. Privacy default.
- Reactions on `authenticated_only` posts are returned only to authed
  callers (the parent post visibility filter already handles this).

Use `requireRole` only if a future privileged action is added; the
three procedures above use `authedProcedure` (add/remove) and
`publicProcedure` (listForPost). Per `api-contract-discipline.md`
rule 7 and the F06 `no-inline-auth-check` rule.

---

## UI states

| State                          | Trigger                              | What user sees                                                       | What user can do                |
| ------------------------------ | ------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |
| Empty                          | No reactions on post                 | Pill with "React" affordance, muted                                  | Tap to open tray                |
| Has reactions, none mine       | Aggregate exists, caller un-reacted  | Pill with top emoji(s) + counts, no highlight                        | Tap to open tray                |
| Has reactions, mine highlighted| Caller has 1+ reactions on post      | Same pill, with picked emoji segments visibly highlighted (ring)     | Tap to open tray                |
| Tray open                      | Tap on pill                          | 8-emoji picker overlays/expands; tray dismisses on outside-click     | Tap an emoji to toggle, or close|
| Adding (optimistic)            | Tap an emoji not yet picked          | Emoji highlights immediately; pill increments; mutation in flight    | Wait briefly                    |
| Add failed                     | Server returned error                | Optimistic UI rolls back; quiet inline error ("Couldn't react")      | Retry                           |
| Removing (optimistic)          | Tap an emoji already picked          | Highlight removes immediately; pill decrements; mutation in flight   | Wait briefly                    |
| Unauthenticated viewing public | No `ctx.user`, post is public        | Pill renders aggregates, tray disabled / shows "Log in to react" CTA | Tap CTA → /dev/login            |

**No state** for "5 people just reacted" celebration. No state for
streak count. No state for "react to unlock something." This is
deliberate per principle 3.

**Per Scenario 3:** the "no system events" rendering is the current
default — reactions don't surface anything in the feed beyond their
counts on the source post.

---

## Tests required

**Integration (real DB):**

1. Toggle add → row exists; toggle add again same emoji → still one
   row (idempotent)
2. Multi-emoji from same user → multiple rows, all returned in
   aggregate with `mine: true`
3. Two users react with the same emoji → count = 2; each sees own
   `mine` correctly
4. Toggle remove → row deleted; cascade on user delete also tested
   (delete the user, check rows gone)

**Unit:**

5. Router `add` rejects unauth callers (`UNAUTHORIZED` error)
6. Router `add` rejects unknown emoji via Zod
7. Router `add` rejects malformed `postId` via Zod
8. `listForPost` aggregates correctly (sort by count, then enum order)
9. `listForPost` returns `mine: false` for un-authed callers

**Manual click-through:**

10. Scenario 3 — log in as a seeded user, find or seed a Shabbat-style
    post, tap reaction pill, pick 🕯️ and 🙏, refresh → both persist;
    tap again to toggle off

**Not required:**

- E2E with real WhatsApp (out of scope; this BU has no dispatch path)
- Performance benchmarks (defer until reactions list is huge in real
  data)
- Visual-regression snapshots (component library is not yet
  visual-regression-tested anywhere)

---

## Scenarios to verify against

See `docs/product/scenarios.md`:

- **Scenario 3 (David reacts to Shabbat post)** — primary target.
  Full multi-select + quiet-acceptance flow.
- **Scenario 1 (Sharon boosts Sky News post)** — partial. The
  "boost" affordance described there is NOT a reaction (it's a share
  + dispatch concept per D017). This BU does not build that. But the
  feed Sharon scrolls through must still render reactions correctly
  on every card. Verify reactions don't break layout for cards
  with AM URLs.
- **Indirect scrutiny:** every other scenario card should render with
  a working reaction pill, even if the scenario doesn't exercise it.

Click through Scenario 3 manually before claiming done.

---

## Known gotchas

- **Polymorphic schema.** `targetType` + `targetId` is forward-
  compatible with comments but the `postId` FK is needed for cascade
  + Prisma relation expression. When BU-comments (Comments) lands, add a
  `commentId` column the same way. Don't try to add `commentId` now
  — YAGNI and untested.
- **Migration name collision.** If anyone else has run a local
  `npx prisma migrate dev`, the `_add_reactions` migration may have
  a different timestamp prefix than what's in the PR. Pull `main`
  fresh before generating.
- **Optimistic UI rollback.** When the mutation fails, the UI must
  revert. React's `useTransition` won't do this automatically;
  manage local state.
- **The `is_comment` analytics property.** The `reaction_added`
  event spec (analytics-events.md:136) carries `is_comment` (bool).
  This BU always fires `is_comment: false`. Surface in open
  questions if the analytics doc should be updated.
- **Self-reaction.** Authors can react to their own posts. Some
  social products gate this; we don't, by default. Open question 2.
- **The 8-emoji set vs analytics-events.md "14 core + 3 seasonal."**
  The parking-lot specifies 8 fixed; analytics-events says 14+3.
  This BU ships 8 per parking-lot. Surface in open questions.
- **Pill placement.** PostCard currently has body, then optional
  AM button in `gps-card__footer`. The pill should land **between**
  body and footer, OR inside the footer flexbox. Surface in open
  questions; ship one and adjust on review.
- **Skipping the AM URL post layout.** Posts without an AM URL
  don't have a footer. The pill must still render. Test both cases
  in the click-through.
- **Custom React component exemption (F14).** `<ReactionPill />` is
  custom; the inner `<button>` it renders gets the testid. Same
  for `<ReactionTray />` — interactive children carry testids.

---

## Decisions confirmed before build (Paul, April 2026)

These are locked. The build session executes against them.

1. **Emoji set: 8 fixed.** Ship the eight emoji listed
   (🕯️🙏❤️💪🎯💕👍😢). Parking-lot already carries the open question
   about expanding/admin-tuning; an additional "Expand reaction set"
   story is parked for after real usage data lands.
2. **Self-reaction allowed.** Authors can react to their own posts.
   No special-case in the router or UI.
3. **Pill placement.** Own row directly below the post body, full
   width minus `var(--space-3)` gutter.
4. **Feature flag `ff_reactions`.** Ship behind a flag per D036.
   Default-on in dev, off in staging until QA confirms. Wired into
   `reaction.add` (server) and `<ReactionPill />` (client).

---

## Open questions still to surface

Things this session cannot decide autonomously. Surface; do not
assume.

5. **Polymorphic shape (`targetType` + `targetId` + `postId`).**
   Forward-compatible with comments (BU-comments) but slightly redundant
   today. Cleaner pattern would be a discriminated union per target
   type, but Prisma doesn't model that natively. Confirm the dual-
   column approach.

6. **Aggregate sort order.** Brief says: by count desc, tie-break
   by enum order. Alternative: by recency of last reaction. The
   first is simpler and stable; the second nudges toward novelty.
   Recommend stable; confirm.

7. **Empty-state copy.** "React" is the current pill label when no
   reactions exist. Sharon-warmth alternatives: "Add a reaction",
   "💕". Recommend literal "React" for the demo (verb in plain
   English per CLAUDE.md "no jargon" guidance).

8. **`is_comment` analytics property.** Always `false` here. When
   BU-comments ships, that property starts varying. Should this BU
   modify `analytics-events.md` to say "currently always false"?
   Recommend: leave the doc alone; it already covers the future
   case.

9. **Visibility on `authenticated_only` posts.** Reactions on those
   posts visible only to authed users — handled by the parent
   `post.list` visibility filter. Confirm: no separate gate needed
   on `reaction.listForPost` if `targetId` is a private post?
   (Recommend: add a defensive check anyway — small cost, clear
   intent.)

10. **Performance — N+1 risk.** `listPosts` will fan out a reactions
    aggregate per post. For 18 seeded posts this is fine; for 1000
    posts it isn't. Recommend a single aggregate query (`groupBy
    postId, emoji`) joined in service code; revisit if a real-world
    feed is loaded. Confirm acceptable.

(Claude Code: add any further judgement calls encountered during
implementation.)

---

## What this brief does NOT cover

(Naming gaps explicitly, per the discipline.)

1. **Comments.** BU-comments. The Reaction primitive is forward-compat
   for comments, but no comment code lands here.
2. **Sharing the post.** That's BU-share-out. The "share to
   WhatsApp" affordance Sharon uses in Scenario 1 is a separate
   future BU.
3. **Dispatch / boost / remove.** BU-dispatch / D017. Distinct from
   reactions.
4. **Notifications on receiving a reaction.** Per design-philosophy
   principle 3. May surface as a parking-lot item later.
5. **Reaction-driven feed ranking.** Feed is chronological today
   (BU-feed scope); reactions feed-rank is a future, evidenced
   change.
6. **Custom emoji or emoji upload.** Fixed set of 8.
7. **Per-user reaction history view (`/me/reactions`).** Out.
8. **Replacing the "boost" concept.** Boost (D017) is a verdict on
   a queue work-item, not a reaction.

---

## Slice convention

BU-reactions is a **feat** session — adds a new entity, new router,
new UI. Commit type `feat(reactions)`. Establishes:

- The Reaction primitive (forward-compatible polymorphic shape)
- The "react" area prefix in the testid + canonical areas
- The pattern of fixed-emoji enums (vs. free-text), to be reused if
  / when other emoji-bearing fields appear
- D047 as the canonical decision record for the reactions schema

Future BUs in this family may include BU-comments (Comments — reuses
Reaction with `targetType=comment`), an admin-tunable emoji set
(parking-lot), and reaction-driven ranking signals.

---

## What lands after this session

- Members can react to any post in the feed with one or more emoji
  from the fixed 8-emoji set
- Reactions render quietly; no celebration; no anxiety-amplifying
  signals
- Scenario 3 demoable end-to-end
- The Reaction primitive is in place for BU-comments to reuse
- The `reaction_added` analytics event is firing in development
- Feature flag `ff_reactions` registered (per D036), allowing the
  feature to be toggled

Next BU candidate (per the post-demo roadmap): BU-comments, OR
BU-composer-fab + urgent-action templates. Decision belongs to
Paul after reactions ships and feels right.
